item.title: High-pressure and magnetism in the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution ${\mathrm{Ca}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{Na}}_{x}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$: A multimodal neutron, muon, and x-ray study
item.description:
Author(s): E. Nocerino, H. Sakurai, O. K. Forslund, K. Papadopoulos, D. J. Mukkattukavil, D. Andreica, G. Simutis, R. Khasanov, N. Ishimatsu, N. Kawamura, C. L. Bull, N. P. Funnell, J. Sugiyama, I. Umegaki, Y. Sassa, and M. Månsson

We investigate the pressure-dependent magnetism of the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution ${\mathrm{Ca}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{Na}}_{x}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$ using neutron diffraction, Cr K-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy, and muon spin rotation/relaxation. Na substitution is known t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104414] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. Nocerino, H. Sakurai, O. K. Forslund, K. Papadopoulos, D. J. Mukkattukavil, D. Andreica, G. Simutis, R. Khasanov, N. Ishimatsu, N. Kawamura, C. L. Bull, N. P. Funnell, J. Sugiyama, I. Umegaki, Y. Sassa, and M. Månsson

We investigate the pressure-dependent magnetism of the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution Ca1−xNaxCr2O4 using neutron diffraction, Cr K-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy, and muon spin rotation/relaxation. Na substitution is known to increase oxygen-ligand hole density (partial Cr3+ to Cr4+ chara…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104414] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: High-pressure and magnetism in the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution ${\mathrm{Ca}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{Na}}_{x}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$: A multimodal neutron, muon, and x-ray study
item.dc:creator: E. Nocerino, H. Sakurai, O. K. Forslund, K. Papadopoulos, D. J. Mukkattukavil, D. Andreica, G. Simutis, R. Khasanov, N. Ishimatsu, N. Kawamura, C. L. Bull, N. P. Funnell, J. Sugiyama, I. Umegaki, Y. Sassa, and M. Månsson

item.title: Quantum geometry in low-energy linear and nonlinear optical responses of the magnetic Rashba semiconductor (Ge,Mn)Te
item.description:
Author(s): Tsubasa Takagi, Hikaru Watanabe, Ryutaro Yoshimi, Yuki Sato, Shingo Toyoda, Atsushi Tsukazaki, Kei S. Takahashi, Masashi Kawasaki, Yoshinori Tokura, and Naoki Ogawa

Quantum geometry appears as a key factor in understanding the optical properties of quantum materials, with the anticipation on diverging or quantized responses near the Dirac and Weyl points. Here, we investigate linear and nonlinear optical responses—optical conductivity and injection current—in a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104417] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tsubasa Takagi, Hikaru Watanabe, Ryutaro Yoshimi, Yuki Sato, Shingo Toyoda, Atsushi Tsukazaki, Kei S. Takahashi, Masashi Kawasaki, Yoshinori Tokura, and Naoki Ogawa

Quantum geometry appears as a key factor in understanding the optical properties of quantum materials, with the anticipation on diverging or quantized responses near the Dirac and Weyl points. Here, we investigate linear and nonlinear optical responses—optical conductivity and injection current—in a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104417] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum geometry in low-energy linear and nonlinear optical responses of the magnetic Rashba semiconductor (Ge,Mn)Te

item.title: Spin polarization and diode effect in thermoelectric current through altermagnet-based superconductor heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Debika Debnath, Arijit Saha, and Paramita Dutta

The recent advent of a new class of magnetic material named as altermagnet (AM), characterized by a combination of momentum-dependent spin splitting with zero net magnetization, has opened up promising prospects for spintronic applications. We theoretically explore how the altermagnetic spin splitti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104508] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Debika Debnath, Arijit Saha, and Paramita Dutta

The recent advent of a new class of magnetic material named as altermagnet (AM), characterized by a combination of momentum-dependent spin splitting with zero net magnetization, has opened up promising prospects for spintronic applications. We theoretically explore how the altermagnetic spin splitti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104508] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026

item.title: Chiral anomaly induced nonlinear transverse planar transport phenomena in three-dimensional spin-orbit coupled metals
item.description:
Author(s): Rishi G. Gopalakrishnan, Binayyak B. Roy, Gargee Sharma, and Sumanta Tewari

We investigate the nonlinear transverse planar transport phenomena (viz., nonlinear Hall, thermal Hall, and Nernst coefficients) induced by chiral anomaly in three-dimensional spin-orbit coupled metallic systems. Unlike Weyl semimetals, these systems do not possess multiple Weyl nodes located at iso…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115409] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rishi G. Gopalakrishnan, Binayyak B. Roy, Gargee Sharma, and Sumanta Tewari

We investigate the nonlinear transverse planar transport phenomena (viz., nonlinear Hall, thermal Hall, and Nernst coefficients) induced by chiral anomaly in three-dimensional spin-orbit coupled metallic systems. Unlike Weyl semimetals, these systems do not possess multiple Weyl nodes located at iso…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115409] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Chiral anomaly induced nonlinear transverse planar transport phenomena in three-dimensional spin-orbit coupled metals

item.title: Emergence of nematic loop-current bond order in kagome metals near van Hove singularities
item.description:
Author(s): Alex Friedlan and Hae-Young Kee

The recently discovered family of kagome metals has attracted significant interest as a result of reports of charge-bond order, orbital magnetism, and superconductivity. Some of these phases may exhibit time-reversal symmetry breaking. More recently, experiments have reported the emergence of nemati…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125118] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alex Friedlan and Hae-Young Kee

The recently discovered family of kagome metals has attracted significant interest as a result of reports of charge-bond order, orbital magnetism, and superconductivity. Some of these phases may exhibit time-reversal symmetry breaking. More recently, experiments have reported the emergence of nemati…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125118] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026

item.title: Magnetic control of Dirac points emerging in carbon nanoscrolls
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Hsiang Fu, Ying-Je Lee, Ching-Hao Chang, and Chao-Cheng Kaun

Dirac points close to the Fermi level can cause geometrical quantities, such as the Berry curvature and quantum metric, to diverge, thereby significantly influencing the transverse electronic responses of the system. Theoretical studies have demonstrated that Dirac points can emerge in the band stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125416] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Hsiang Fu, Ying-Je Lee, Ching-Hao Chang, and Chao-Cheng Kaun

Dirac points close to the Fermi level can cause geometrical quantities, such as the Berry curvature and quantum metric, to diverge, thereby significantly influencing the transverse electronic responses of the system. Theoretical studies have demonstrated that Dirac points can emerge in the band stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125416] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026

item.title: Large violation of the Wiedemann-Franz law driven by electron-hole compensation in the topological semimetal CoSi
item.description:
Author(s): Luyao Zhong, Xin Jin, Mingquan He, Rui Wang, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Tianqi Deng, and Xiaolong Yang

The Wiedemann-Franz (WF) law, relating the electronic thermal conductivity (${κ}_{\mathrm{e}}$) to the electrical conductivity, is vital in numerous applications such as in the design of thermoelectric materials and in the experimental determination of the lattice thermal conductivity (${κ}_{\mathrm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121102] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luyao Zhong, Xin Jin, Mingquan He, Rui Wang, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Tianqi Deng, and Xiaolong Yang

The Wiedemann-Franz (WF) law, relating the electronic thermal conductivity (κe) to the electrical conductivity, is vital in numerous applications such as in the design of thermoelectric materials and in the experimental determination of the lattice thermal conductivity (κL). While the WF law is gene…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121102] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Large violation of the Wiedemann-Franz law driven by electron-hole compensation in the topological semimetal CoSi

item.title: Temperature-dependent charge density waves in $1H\text{−}{\mathrm{TaSe}}_{2}$ through anharmonic lattice energetics
item.description:
Author(s): Sohee Park, Young-Kyun Kwon, and Changwon Park

Charge density waves (CDWs), spontaneous modulations of charge and lattice, are one of the fundamental orders in condensed matter systems. Since the modulation period of CDW strongly modifies the electronic structure and influences other emergent orders, unraveling the mechanism responsible for the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094106] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sohee Park, Young-Kyun Kwon, and Changwon Park

Charge density waves (CDWs), spontaneous modulations of charge and lattice, are one of the fundamental orders in condensed matter systems. Since the modulation period of CDW strongly modifies the electronic structure and influences other emergent orders, unraveling the mechanism responsible for the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094106] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.title: Detecting quantum spin liquid on Kitaev model through a superconducting junction
item.description:
Author(s): A. R. Moura and L. V. Santos

The Kitaev model belongs to an unconventional class of two-dimensional spin systems characterized by anisotropic, bond-dependent interactions that give rise to quantum spin liquid (QSL) states. These exotic phases, marked by the absence of magnetic ordering even at zero temperature, support fraction…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094413] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. R. Moura and L. V. Santos

The Kitaev model belongs to an unconventional class of two-dimensional spin systems characterized by anisotropic, bond-dependent interactions that give rise to quantum spin liquid (QSL) states. These exotic phases, marked by the absence of magnetic ordering even at zero temperature, support fraction…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094413] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.title: Strain-enhanced edge ferromagnetism and bipolar magnetic semiconducting behavior in Janus graphene nanoribbons
item.description:
Author(s): Ran Liu, Hongxing Liu, Junfeng Ren, and Tianxing Ma

Using first-principles density functional theory and determinant quantum Monte Carlo methods, we show that Janus graphene nanoribbons with topological defect arrays ($m=2$) exhibit robust intrinsic ferromagnetism across widths $W=2\text{–}6$, with bandgaps exceeding $200\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094414] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ran Liu, Hongxing Liu, Junfeng Ren, and Tianxing Ma

Using first-principles density functional theory and determinant quantum Monte Carlo methods, we show that Janus graphene nanoribbons with topological defect arrays (m=2) exhibit robust intrinsic ferromagnetism across widths W=2–6, with bandgaps exceeding 200meV and stable ferromagnetic ground state…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094414] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain-enhanced edge ferromagnetism and bipolar magnetic semiconducting behavior in Janus graphene nanoribbons

item.title: Pressure-induced evolution of superconductivity and topological electronic structure in ${\mathrm{LaNiGa}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yikang Li, Ye Yang, Houpu Li, Yuqing Zhang, Yanjun Li, Qingyuan Liu, Rongqi Wu, Xiaopei Cao, Rui Wang, Jianjun Ying, and Xianhui Chen

${\mathrm{LaNiGa}}_{2}$, as a time-reversal symmetry breaking superconductor, has garnered significant attention due to distinctive Dirac points and nonunitary triplet pairing. Here, we investigate the superconducting properties of ${\mathrm{LaNiGa}}_{2}$ under high pressure and uncover a dome-like …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104505] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yikang Li, Ye Yang, Houpu Li, Yuqing Zhang, Yanjun Li, Qingyuan Liu, Rongqi Wu, Xiaopei Cao, Rui Wang, Jianjun Ying, and Xianhui Chen

LaNiGa2, as a time-reversal symmetry breaking superconductor, has garnered significant attention due to distinctive Dirac points and nonunitary triplet pairing. Here, we investigate the superconducting properties of LaNiGa2 under high pressure and uncover a dome-like behavior in its superconducting …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104505] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-induced evolution of superconductivity and topological electronic structure in ${\mathrm{LaNiGa}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Yikang Li, Ye Yang, Houpu Li, Yuqing Zhang, Yanjun Li, Qingyuan Liu, Rongqi Wu, Xiaopei Cao, Rui Wang, Jianjun Ying, and Xianhui Chen

item.title: Dirac bilinears in condensed matter physics: Relativistic correction for observables and conjugate electromagnetic fields
item.description:
Author(s): Shintaro Hoshino, Tatsuya Miki, Michi-To Suzuki, and Hiroaki Ikeda

Inspired by recent developments in electron chirality, we reconsider some microscopic physical quantities that have been overlooked or have received little attention in condensed-matter physics, based on the nonrelativistic limit of the Dirac bilinears in relativistic quantum theory. We identify the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115113] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shintaro Hoshino, Tatsuya Miki, Michi-To Suzuki, and Hiroaki Ikeda

Inspired by recent developments in electron chirality, we reconsider some microscopic physical quantities that have been overlooked or have received little attention in condensed-matter physics, based on the nonrelativistic limit of the Dirac bilinears in relativistic quantum theory. We identify the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115113] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.title: Spin and charge transport mediated by fractionalized excitations
item.description:
Author(s): Alexey Ermakov and Alessandro Principi

We study charge and spin transport in systems composed of itinerant electrons and localized magnetic moments of a Kitaev quantum spin liquid (QSL) phase. For example, $α\text{−}{\mathrm{RuCl}}_{3}$ either is intrinsically doped or in proximity to graphene. Our analysis reveals distinct temperature-d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115115] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alexey Ermakov and Alessandro Principi

We study charge and spin transport in systems composed of itinerant electrons and localized magnetic moments of a Kitaev quantum spin liquid (QSL) phase. For example, α−RuCl3 either is intrinsically doped or in proximity to graphene. Our analysis reveals distinct temperature-dependent transport beha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115115] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin and charge transport mediated by fractionalized excitations

item.title: Dynamic order of a quantum spin liquid at nonzero temperatures
item.description:
Author(s): Minsu Park, Masafumi Udagawa, and Eun-Gook Moon

A quantum spin liquid hosts massive quantum entanglement whose identification is one of the most significant problems in physics. Yet, its detection is known to be notoriously difficult because of featureless properties without a symmetry order parameter. Here, we demonstrate dynamic signatures of a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115118] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minsu Park, Masafumi Udagawa, and Eun-Gook Moon

A quantum spin liquid hosts massive quantum entanglement whose identification is one of the most significant problems in physics. Yet, its detection is known to be notoriously difficult because of featureless properties without a symmetry order parameter. Here, we demonstrate dynamic signatures of a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115118] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.title: Nonmagnetic vacancy engineering for enhanced spin-orbit and valley splitting in $\mathrm{W}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}/{\mathrm{CrBr}}_{3}$ van der Waals heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Danyang Guo, Yongsheng Zhao, Haishan Zhang, Juan Lyu, Jian Gong, and Shaoqiang Guo

The development of predictive theories for spin and valley properties in $\mathrm{W}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}/{\mathrm{CrBr}}_{3}$ heterostructures requires fundamental understanding of nonmagnetic vacancy effects. Using density functional theory calculations, we find that nonmagnetic vacancies significantly…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115305] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Danyang Guo, Yongsheng Zhao, Haishan Zhang, Juan Lyu, Jian Gong, and Shaoqiang Guo

The development of predictive theories for spin and valley properties in WS2/CrBr3 heterostructures requires fundamental understanding of nonmagnetic vacancy effects. Using density functional theory calculations, we find that nonmagnetic vacancies significantly enhance the spin-orbit splitting at th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115305] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonmagnetic vacancy engineering for enhanced spin-orbit and valley splitting in $\mathrm{W}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}/{\mathrm{CrBr}}_{3}$ van der Waals heterostructures

item.title: Emergence of Hermitian topology from non-Hermitian knots
item.description:
Author(s): Gaurav Hajong, Ranjan Modak, and Bhabani Prasad Mandal

The non-Hermiticity of the system gives rise to a distinct knot topology in the complex eigenvalue spectrum, which has no counterpart in Hermitian systems. In contrast, the singular values of a non-Hermitian (NH) Hamiltonian are always real by definition, meaning that they can also be interpreted as…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115406] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gaurav Hajong, Ranjan Modak, and Bhabani Prasad Mandal

The non-Hermiticity of the system gives rise to a distinct knot topology in the complex eigenvalue spectrum, which has no counterpart in Hermitian systems. In contrast, the singular values of a non-Hermitian (NH) Hamiltonian are always real by definition, meaning that they can also be interpreted as…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115406] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.title: Unsupervised learning for non-Hermitian systems with higher-order hopping
item.description:
Author(s): Taeyoon Kim, Yihao Xu, Yuxiao Li, Alexander Montes McNeil, Allen Zhang, Michael Moebius, Sunil Mittal, and Yongmin Liu

Machine learning offers a promising approach to addressing the unique challenges of non-Hermitian systems, particularly in classifying topological phases and overcoming the limitations of traditional methods. However, research remains sparse for complex non-Hermitian systems involving higher-order h…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115407] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:

Machine learning offers a promising approach to addressing the unique challenges of non-Hermitian systems, particularly in classifying topological phases and overcoming the limitations of traditional methods. However, research remains sparse for complex non-Hermitian systems involving higher-order h…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115407] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.title: Valley-selective linear dichroism and excitonic effects in Lieb-lattice altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Haonan Wang, Xilong Xu, Du Li, and Li Yang

Altermagnets have recently been recognized as a distinct class of magnetic materials characterized by alternative spin-split electronic structures without net magnetization. Despite intensive studies on their single-particle spintronic and valleytronic properties, many-electron interactions and opti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115408] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

Altermagnets have recently been recognized as a distinct class of magnetic materials characterized by alternative spin-split electronic structures without net magnetization. Despite intensive studies on their single-particle spintronic and valleytronic properties, many-electron interactions and opti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115408] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Valley-selective linear dichroism and excitonic effects in Lieb-lattice altermagnets

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Spin texture of $α\text{−}\mathrm{GeTe}$ in the ultrathin regime
item.description:
Author(s): Calvin Tagne-Kaegom, Alexandre Llopez, Boris Croes, Geoffroy Kremer, Bertrand Kierren, Daniel Malterre, Luc Moreau, Fotié Jaurès Ngoufo, Stefano Curiotto, Pierre Müller, Patrick Le Fèvre, Julien Rault, François Bertran, Andrés Saúl, Frédéric Leroy, Fabien Cheynis, and Yannick Fagot-Revurat

Ferroelectric Rashba semiconductors such as α-GeTe have recently emerged as promising candidates for all-electrically controlled spin orbitronics. The persistence of Rashba spin-split electronic states in ultrathin α-GeTe films is of utmost importance in the context of device downscaling. Using spin- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, supported by first-principles calculations, the authors demonstrate here that α-GeTe films as thin as 5 nm still exhibit an in-plane spin texture at the Fermi surface.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125113] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Calvin Tagne-Kaegom, Alexandre Llopez, Boris Croes, Geoffroy Kremer, Bertrand Kierren, Daniel Malterre, Luc Moreau, Fotié Jaurès Ngoufo, Stefano Curiotto, Pierre Müller, Patrick Le Fèvre, Julien Rault, François Bertran, Andrés Saúl, Frédéric Leroy, Fabien Cheynis, and Yannick Fagot-Revurat

Ferroelectric Rashba semiconductors such as α-GeTe have recently emerged as promising candidates for all-electrically controlled spin orbitronics. The persistence of Rashba spin-split electronic states in ultrathin α-GeTe films is of utmost importance in the context of device downscaling. Using spin- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, supported by first-principles calculations, the authors demonstrate here that α-GeTe films as thin as 5 nm still exhibit an in-plane spin texture at the Fermi surface.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125113] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin texture of $α\text{−}\mathrm{GeTe}$ in the ultrathin regime
item.dc:creator: Calvin Tagne-Kaegom, Alexandre Llopez, Boris Croes, Geoffroy Kremer, Bertrand Kierren, Daniel Malterre, Luc Moreau, Fotié Jaurès Ngoufo, Stefano Curiotto, Pierre Müller, Patrick Le Fèvre, Julien Rault, François Bertran, Andrés Saúl, Frédéric Leroy, Fabien Cheynis, and Yannick Fagot-Revurat
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Chern-selective multivalley flat bands in twisted mono-bilayer and mono-trilayer ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Ziyue Qi, Hanqi Pi, Yan Zhang, Jiaxuan Liu, Nicolas Regnault, Hongming Weng, B. Andrei Bernevig, Jiabin Yu, and Quansheng Wu

Here, the authors show that in twisted mono-bilayer and mono-trilayer MoTe2, moirapose flat bands from multiple valleys, with distinct spin Chern numbers, emerge simultaneously at low energies. Using first-principles calculations and continuum models, they reveal that interlayer hybridization enables stacking configuration and thickness to tune the relative alignment and quantum geometry of low-energy moirapose bands from K/K′ and Γ valleys. These results provide promising platforms to study layer-controlled multivalley physics and call for extended investigations of twisted multilayer heterostructures to explore richer multivalley phenomena.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125116] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ziyue Qi, Hanqi Pi, Yan Zhang, Jiaxuan Liu, Nicolas Regnault, Hongming Weng, B. Andrei Bernevig, Jiabin Yu, and Quansheng Wu

Here, the authors show that in twisted mono-bilayer and mono-trilayer MoTe2, moirapose flat bands from multiple valleys, with distinct spin Chern numbers, emerge simultaneously at low energies. Using first-principles calculations and continuum models, they reveal that interlayer hybridization enables stacking configuration and thickness to tune the relative alignment and quantum geometry of low-energy moirapose bands from K/K′ and Γ valleys. These results provide promising platforms to study layer-controlled multivalley physics and call for extended investigations of twisted multilayer heterostructures to explore richer multivalley phenomena.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125116] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Chern-selective multivalley flat bands in twisted mono-bilayer and mono-trilayer ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Ziyue Qi, Hanqi Pi, Yan Zhang, Jiaxuan Liu, Nicolas Regnault, Hongming Weng, B. Andrei Bernevig, Jiabin Yu, and Quansheng Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Fusion rule in conformal field theories and topological orders: A unified view of correspondence and (fractional) supersymmetry and their relation to topological holography
item.description:
Author(s): Yoshiki Fukusumi

The algebraic or ring structure of anyons, called the fusion rule, is one of the most fundamental research interests in contemporary studies on topological orders (TOs) and the corresponding conformal field theories (CFTs). Recently, the algebraic structure realized as generalized symmetry, includin…

item.title: Chern insulators in two and three dimensions: A global perspective
item.description:
Author(s): Jason G. Kattan and J. E. Sipe

We introduce a second-quantized field theory for Chern insulators in which the Hamiltonian features a static vector potential that has the periodicity of the crystal's lattice and spontaneously breaks time-reversal symmetry in the system's ground state. Such a vector potential generates a magnetic f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125201] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

Such a vector potential generates a magnetic f…

item.title: All-order dark mode plasmon-induced transparency underpinned by transitional resonance phase
item.description:
Author(s): Guizi Qing, Jiali Huang, Di Zhang, Xiang Zhai, Guidong Liu, and Sheng-Xuan Xia

We demonstrate a graphene nanoribbon (GNR) metagrating on a rectangular dielectric ridge that exhibits plasmonically induced transparency (PIT) through the excitation of both odd- and even-order dark modes. The metagrating integrates a top bright mode GNR coupled directly to light and a sidewall dar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125413] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

We demonstrate a graphene nanoribbon (GNR) metagrating on a rectangular dielectric ridge that exhibits plasmonically induced transparency (PIT) through the excitation of both odd- and even-order dark modes. The metagrating integrates a top bright mode GNR coupled directly to light and a sidewall dar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125413] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Two-stage dynamics and phase control of skyrmion formation in chiral magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Shiwei Zhu, Xinyuan Guan, Zhen Sun, Qiuyao Zhang, and Changsheng Song

The authors uncover here a two-stage dynamical process for skyrmion formation in chiral magnets. Atomistic spin simulations show that stripe domains first coarsen and then contract into skyrmions, with exchange, anisotropy, and magnetic field playing distinct roles in the two stages. By combining neural networks with symbolic regression, the authors extract an interpretable, field-dependent criterion that predicts topological phases and provides a microscopic roadmap for controlling skyrmion creation.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100403] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shiwei Zhu, Xinyuan Guan, Zhen Sun, Qiuyao Zhang, and Changsheng Song

The authors uncover here a two-stage dynamical process for skyrmion formation in chiral magnets. Atomistic spin simulations show that stripe domains first coarsen and then contract into skyrmions, with exchange, anisotropy, and magnetic field playing distinct roles in the two stages. By combining neural networks with symbolic regression, the authors extract an interpretable, field-dependent criterion that predicts topological phases and provides a microscopic roadmap for controlling skyrmion creation.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100403] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-stage dynamics and phase control of skyrmion formation in chiral magnets
item.dc:creator: Shiwei Zhu, Xinyuan Guan, Zhen Sun, Qiuyao Zhang, and Changsheng Song

item.title: Dynamical scarring from scrambling in two-dimensional topological materials
item.description:
Author(s): Dominik Szpara, Szczepan Głodzik, and Nicholas Sedlmayr

Out-of-time ordered correlators are a probe of how the information of an initial perturbation is effectively scrambled under unitary time evolution, widely used to study quantum chaos. They have also been used to demonstrate that information is trapped in the zero-dimensional edge modes of topologic…

However, the fundamental mechanism governing ASks formation in two-dimensional systems is rarely explored. Here, we demonstrate that the MX2 (NbO2, NbS2, NbSe2, …

item.title: Magnetic ground state of atacamite ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}\mathrm{Cl}{(\mathrm{OH})}_{3}$: The crucial role of frustrated zigzag chains revealed by inelastic neutron scattering
item.description:
Author(s): J. L. Allen, L. Stödter, R. A. Mole, S. Süllow, O. Janson, S. Nishimoto, R. A. Lewis, and K. C. Rule

We report inelastic neutron scattering (INS) measurements on the magnetically frustrated $S=\frac{1}{2}$ sawtooth-chain compound atacamite ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}\mathrm{Cl}{(\mathrm{OH})}_{3}$ featuring inequivalent Cu(1) and Cu(2) sites. Transverse to the sawtooth chains, INS reveals two dispersive spi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104410] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. L. Allen, L. Stödter, R. A. Mole, S. Süllow, O. Janson, S. Nishimoto, R. A. Lewis, and K. C. Rule

We report inelastic neutron scattering (INS) measurements on the magnetically frustrated S=12 sawtooth-chain compound atacamite Cu2Cl(OH)3 featuring inequivalent Cu(1) and Cu(2) sites. Transverse to the sawtooth chains, INS reveals two dispersive spin-wave modes and a gap of at least 0.75 meV. This …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104410] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026

item.title: Percolative instabilities and sparse-limit fractality in $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Poulomi Maji, Md Aquib Molla, Koushik Dey, Bikash Das, Sambit Choudhury, Tanima Kundu, Pabitra Kumar Hazra, Mainak Palit, Sujan Maity, Bipul Karmakar, Kai Rossnagel, Sanjoy Kr Mahatha, Bhaskaran Muralidharan, Shamashis Sengupta, Sanchari Goswami, and Subhadeep Datta

The low-temperature metallic phase of $1\mathrm{T}\text{−}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$ may originate from the current- and voltage-driven destabilization of the commensurate charge density wave in a strongly correlated Mott-insulator, alongside the robust yet rarely realized influence of intrinsic electronic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115110] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Poulomi Maji, Md Aquib Molla, Koushik Dey, Bikash Das, Sambit Choudhury, Tanima Kundu, Pabitra Kumar Hazra, Mainak Palit, Sujan Maity, Bipul Karmakar, Kai Rossnagel, Sanjoy Kr Mahatha, Bhaskaran Muralidharan, Shamashis Sengupta, Sanchari Goswami, and Subhadeep Datta

The low-temperature metallic phase of 1T−TaS2 may originate from the current- and voltage-driven destabilization of the commensurate charge density wave in a strongly correlated Mott-insulator, alongside the robust yet rarely realized influence of intrinsic electronic distortions. Electrical pulse-d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115110] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026

Author(s): Jian-Jian Miao and Wei-Qiang Chen

Motivated by the order fractionalization in the Kitaev-Kondo model, we propose an exactly solvable spin-charge ladder model to study order fractionalization with discrete symmetry. The spin-charge ladder is composed of a spin chain and a superconducting wire coupled via an Ising-type interaction, an…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125110] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026

The spin-charge ladder is composed of a spin chain and a superconducting wire coupled via an Ising-type interaction, an…

item.title: Laser-induced topological phases in monolayer amorphous carbon
item.description:
Author(s): Arnob Kumar Ghosh, Quentin Marsal, and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Driving nontopological materials out of equilibrium using time-periodic perturbations, such as circularly polarized laser light, is a compelling way to engineer topological phases. At the same time, topology has traditionally only been considered for crystalline materials. Here we propose an experim…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121402] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arnob Kumar Ghosh, Quentin Marsal, and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Driving nontopological materials out of equilibrium using time-periodic perturbations, such as circularly polarized laser light, is a compelling way to engineer topological phases. At the same time, topology has traditionally only been considered for crystalline materials. Here we propose an experim…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121402] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Laser-induced topological phases in monolayer amorphous carbon

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Magnetic excitations in the Kitaev material ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}{\mathrm{IrO}}_{3}$ studied by neutron scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Alexandre Bertin, Hengdi Zhao, Gang Cao, Andrea Piovano, Paul Steffens, Alexandre Ivanov, and Markus Braden

Na2IrO3 is one of the most studied Kitaev materials, but the microscopic interactions remain controversial leaving the imminent question open, whether Na2IrO3 shows or approaches a quantum spin liquid phase. The authors overcome here the challenges to perform neutron scattering experiments on highly absorbing Na2IrO3 by aligning a large number of thin crystals. These experiments reveal a small gap in the magnetic excitations similar to the sister compound α-RuCl3. However, Na2IrO3 does not exhibit low-energy ferromagnetic excitations reflecting nearest-neighbor Kitaev and Heisenberg interaction terms with opposite signs.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094407] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alexandre Bertin, Hengdi Zhao, Gang Cao, Andrea Piovano, Paul Steffens, Alexandre Ivanov, and Markus Braden

Na2IrO3 is one of the most studied Kitaev materials, but the microscopic interactions remain controversial leaving the imminent question open, whether Na2IrO3 shows or approaches a quantum spin liquid phase. The authors overcome here the challenges to perform neutron scattering experiments on highly absorbing Na2IrO3 by aligning a large number of thin crystals. These experiments reveal a small gap in the magnetic excitations similar to the sister compound α-RuCl3. However, Na2IrO3 does not exhibit low-energy ferromagnetic excitations reflecting nearest-neighbor Kitaev and Heisenberg interaction terms with opposite signs.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094407] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic excitations in the Kitaev material ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}{\mathrm{IrO}}_{3}$ studied by neutron scattering
item.dc:creator: Alexandre Bertin, Hengdi Zhao, Gang Cao, Andrea Piovano, Paul Steffens, Alexandre Ivanov, and Markus Braden
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Systematic study of superconductivity in few-layer ${T}_{d}{\text{-MoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): T. Wakamura, M. Hashisaka, Y. Nomura, M. Bard, S. Okazaki, T. Sasagawa, T. Taniguchi, K. Watanabe, K. Muraki, and N. Kumada

We present a systematic investigation of superconductivity in a topological superconductor candidate ${T}_{d}{\text{-MoTe}}_{2}$ in the few-layer limit. By examining multiple mechanically exfoliated samples with different thicknesses, substrates, and crystal qualities, we quantitatively correlate su…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094503] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Wakamura, M. Hashisaka, Y. Nomura, M. Bard, S. Okazaki, T. Sasagawa, T. Taniguchi, K. Watanabe, K. Muraki, and N. Kumada

We present a systematic investigation of superconductivity in a topological superconductor candidate Td-MoTe2 in the few-layer limit. By examining multiple mechanically exfoliated samples with different thicknesses, substrates, and crystal qualities, we quantitatively correlate superconducting tempe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094503] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Systematic study of superconductivity in few-layer ${T}_{d}{\text{-MoTe}}_{2}$

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Multiorbital Dirac superconductors and their realization of higher-order topology
item.description:
Author(s): Dao-He Ma and Jin An

Topological nodal superconductors (SCs) have attracted considerable interest due to their gapless bulk excitations and exotic surface states. In this paper, by establishing a general framework of the effective theory for multiorbital SCs in the weak-pairing limit, we realize a class of three-dimensi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104503] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dao-He Ma and Jin An

Topological nodal superconductors (SCs) have attracted considerable interest due to their gapless bulk excitations and exotic surface states. In this paper, by establishing a general framework of the effective theory for multiorbital SCs in the weak-pairing limit, we realize a class of three-dimensi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104503] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
